title: Cat in the Hat Celebration Day!
story: Every March 2, schools across America turn into wonderlands of rhymes and silly creatures! Dr. Seuss Day celebrates the birthday of Theodor Seuss Geisel, the genius behind beloved books like "Green Eggs and Ham" and "The Cat in the Hat." Teachers wear striped hats, kids dress up as Truffula Trees and Sneetches, and classrooms buzz with read-alouds of zany stories. Libraries host special story times where everyone speaks in rhyme, and some schools even serve green eggs for lunch! It's a day when imagination runs wild and reading becomes pure magic.
activity_question: What do some schools serve for lunch on Dr. Seuss Day?
bonus_fact: Dr. Seuss wrote "Green Eggs and Ham" using only 50 different words!

title: Books Take Over America!
story: Every March 2, schools across America turn into reading wonderlands for Read Across America Day! Teachers dress up as favorite book characters, from the Cat in the Hat to Harry Potter. Students bring blankets and stuffed animals for cozy reading time. Libraries host special story hours with puppet shows and book giveaways. Many classrooms have reading marathons where kids try to read as many books as possible in one day.
activity_question: What do teachers often do on Read Across America Day?
follow_up_question: What book character would you most want your teacher to dress up as?

title: Going Bananas for Cream Pie Day!
story: Bakeries across America whip up their fluffiest banana cream pies every March 2 for National Banana Cream Pie Day! This dreamy dessert features layers of vanilla custard, fresh banana slices, and towering whipped cream all nestled in a buttery crust. Families gather to bake homemade versions, while restaurants offer special deals on their signature pies. Social media fills with photos of people's gooey, golden creations. Some brave bakers even attempt sky-high versions with extra banana layers. Whether you buy one from your favorite diner or make it from scratch, this holiday gives everyone the perfect excuse to indulge in this classic American comfort dessert.
activity_question: What are the main ingredients that make up a traditional banana cream pie?
activity_choices: Vanilla custard, banana slices, whipped cream, and crust
Chocolate pudding, strawberries, and graham crackers
Apple filling, cinnamon, and pie crust
Peanut butter, jelly, and bread crumbs
activity_answer: Vanilla custard, banana slices, whipped cream, and crust
bonus_fact: Banana cream pie became popular in the 1950s when bananas were finally affordable year-round!

title: National Old Stuff Day: Treasures and Trash
story: Every March 2, people celebrate National Old Stuff Day by digging through attics, basements, and closets to rediscover forgotten treasures! Some families spend the day sorting through old photo albums, trying on vintage clothes, or playing with toys they haven't touched in years. Others visit antique shops, flea markets, or garage sales to hunt for cool old items. It's amazing what stories old stuff can tell about the past!
activity_question: What do people often search through on National Old Stuff Day?
follow_up_question: What's the oldest thing you own that tells an interesting story?
